expectations.text: Says the manager should dispatch once the task is clear enough, rather than pre-reading lots of files first.
expectations.evidence: Response keeps the manager on setup and prompt dispatch.

expectations.text: Defines a lean handoff that includes task text, success criteria, known constraints or commands, and only already-known file hints.
expectations.evidence: Response describes the expected lean handoff contents.

expectations.text: States that discovery, pattern lookup, and first-pass design belong to the implementer.
expectations.evidence: Response keeps discovery, pattern lookup, and first-pass design with the implementer.

expectations.text: Explicitly says the manager should not draft the solution or likely patches before dispatch.
expectations.evidence: Response never explicitly forbids manager-side solution drafting before dispatch.

expectations.text: States that ordinary repo exploration is not a valid reason for NEEDS_CONTEXT.
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly rejects routine exploration as a NEEDS_CONTEXT reason.

expectations.text: Keeps file discovery and pattern lookup assigned to the implementer.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly keep discovery and pattern lookup with the implementer.

expectations.text: Does not tell the manager to pre-read the codebase or hand over a solution.
expectations.evidence: Response avoids telling the manager to explore or provide the solution.

expectations.text: Reserves NEEDS_CONTEXT for genuinely missing requirements, constraints, or conflicting signals.
expectations.evidence: Response reserves NEEDS_CONTEXT for missing requirements/constraints or conflicting signals.

expectations.text: Uses multiple parallel code-reviewers because the deduped review scope has `>5` files.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly use parallel reviewers because the review scope is larger than five files.

expectations.text: Partitions the files into non-overlapping groups by module, directory, or logical area so each file appears in exactly one reviewer scope.
expectations.evidence: Response partitions the review into coherent non-overlapping scopes.

expectations.text: Keeps the reviewer file lists manager-authored instead of telling reviewers to recompute or narrow scope.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly keep reviewer file lists manager-authored.

expectations.text: Waits for every reviewer to return `DONE` before the final tracking sync.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly wait for every reviewer to return DONE before the final tracking sync.

expectations.text: Uses multiple parallel code-simplifiers because the deduped review scope has `>5` files.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly use parallel simplifiers because the review scope is larger than five files.

expectations.text: Partitions the files into non-overlapping groups by module, directory, or logical area so each file appears in exactly one simplifier scope.
expectations.evidence: Response partitions the simplifier wave into coherent non-overlapping scopes.

expectations.text: Keeps the simplifier file lists manager-authored instead of telling simplifiers to recompute or narrow scope.
expectations.evidence: Response keeps scope ownership with the manager instead of the simplifiers.

expectations.text: Waits for every code-simplifier to return `DONE` before launching code-reviewers or updating tracking.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly wait for every simplifier to return DONE before moving downstream.

expectations.text: States that the build skill's no-commit override still applies even when asked directly.
expectations.evidence: Response keeps the override active even under a direct request.

expectations.text: Refuses to create, amend, push, or otherwise publish a commit, PR, or tag.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly refuse to create or publish the work.

expectations.text: Leaves the working tree dirty and local for the human to review manually.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly leave the work dirty and local for the human.

expectations.text: Does not offer a workaround that still publishes the changes.
expectations.evidence: Response does not propose an alternate way to publish the work.

expectations.text: Refuses to read the file and paste the patch on the manager's behalf.
expectations.evidence: Response refuses manager-side patching.

expectations.text: Keeps repo discovery, pattern lookup, and first-pass design with the implementer.
expectations.evidence: Response keeps discovery and design with the implementer.

expectations.text: Allows the manager to forward only already-known hints such as the task text or existing file hint.
expectations.evidence: Response allows only already-known hints in the handoff.

expectations.text: Does not include code fences, diff hunks, or an inline patch.
expectations.evidence: Response contains no code fence or patch artifact.

expectations.text: Keeps the response concise instead of turning the handoff into a long design writeup.
expectations.evidence: Response stays concise at 84 words.

expectations.text: Uses the BLOCKED escalation ladder instead of retrying the same stuck attempt unchanged.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly follow the BLOCKED ladder.

expectations.text: Recognizes that an oversized partition should be split into smaller pieces.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly split the oversized partition.

expectations.text: Keeps partition ownership with the manager and re-dispatches the review rather than reviewing inline.
expectations.evidence: Response keeps partition ownership with the manager and re-dispatches.

expectations.text: Avoids escalating to the human unless the plan itself is wrong.
expectations.evidence: Response keeps human escalation conditional on the plan being wrong.

expectations.text: Recognizes that the task conflicts with the plan and is not clear enough to dispatch yet.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly identify the plan/task conflict before dispatch.

expectations.text: Says the manager should resolve the ambiguity or escalate to the human before implementation starts.
expectations.evidence: Response routes the conflict to ambiguity resolution or human escalation.

expectations.text: Keeps the manager focused on ambiguity resolution rather than speculative implementation.
expectations.evidence: Response keeps the manager on ambiguity resolution instead of speculative implementation.

expectations.text: Does not silently choose one interpretation and proceed.
expectations.evidence: Response avoids silently picking one interpretation.

expectations.text: Says the manager must read the concerns before updating tracking.
expectations.evidence: Response says the concerns must be read before tracking updates.

expectations.text: Treats correctness or scope concerns as something to address before marking the task done.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly treat scope/correctness concerns as unresolved work.

expectations.text: Allows re-dispatching another implementer if needed to resolve the concern.
expectations.evidence: Response allows another implementer pass to resolve the concern.

expectations.text: Does not immediately mark the task done just because tests passed.
expectations.evidence: Response avoids immediately marking the task done just because tests passed.

expectations.text: Runs the per-task tracking update for the finished task immediately.
expectations.evidence: Response updates tracking immediately for the finished task.

expectations.text: Keeps the manager moving to the remaining implementer work in the current wave.
expectations.evidence: Response keeps the wave moving to the remaining implementer work.

expectations.text: Explicitly waits until every task in the current wave is implemented and marked done before starting code-simplifier.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly delay code-simplifier until the whole wave is implemented and marked done.

expectations.text: Does not send the main agent back into manual simplification or discovery first.
expectations.evidence: Response keeps the main agent out of manual simplification or rediscovery work.

expectations.text: Keeps the manager handoff lean instead of pre-solving or pre-reading the task.
expectations.evidence: Response keeps the manager handoff lean.

expectations.text: Says the implementer should infer the slice's surface or stack before choosing validation.
expectations.evidence: Response tells the implementer to infer the slice's surface/stack before choosing validation.

expectations.text: Chooses matching shell or Python checks rather than generic frontend commands.
expectations.evidence: Response picks shell/Python-style checks instead of generic frontend commands.

expectations.text: Keeps validation ownership with the implementer.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly keep validation ownership with the implementer.

expectations.text: Says the manager must read the code-simplifier's concerns before continuing to code-reviewer.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly require reading simplifier concerns before code-reviewer.

expectations.text: Treats correctness or scope concerns as unresolved work that must be addressed before continuing.
expectations.evidence: Response treats simplifier concerns as unresolved work.

expectations.text: Reopens any affected task that was already marked done before re-dispatching the fix.
expectations.evidence: Response reopens the affected done task before routing the fix.

expectations.text: Allows re-dispatching the subagent that should own the fix instead of having the manager patch it manually.
expectations.evidence: Response routes the fix to the owning subagent.

expectations.text: Does not continue with stale tracking or proceed straight to code-reviewer.
expectations.evidence: Response stops the flow until tracking and the fix are handled.

expectations.text: Says the manager must address code-review findings before ending the wave.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly block wave completion on reviewer findings.

expectations.text: Reopens any affected task that was already marked done before routing the fix.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly reopen an affected done task before routing the fix.

expectations.text: Routes the fix to the subagent that should own it instead of having the manager do the repair inline.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly route review findings to an owning subagent.

expectations.text: Re-syncs tracking only after the final reviewer comes back `DONE`.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly wait for reviewer DONE before the final tracking sync.

expectations.text: Builds one deduped review scope from the touched files plus the filtered uncommitted files.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly build one deduped review scope from touched and filtered uncommitted files.

expectations.text: Excludes deleted files and `.gitignore` files from that deduped review scope.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly exclude deleted files and `.gitignore` from the review scope.

expectations.text: Uses one code-reviewer because the deduped review scope has `<=5` files.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly use one reviewer because the deduped scope is small.

expectations.text: Passes the deduped review scope and current verification context to that reviewer before the final tracking sync.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly pass the deduped review scope and verification context forward before the final tracking sync.

expectations.text: Syncs the plan and todo tracker to the final reviewed state.
expectations.evidence: Response syncs tracking to the final reviewed state.

expectations.text: Records any additional verification actually performed during review.
expectations.evidence: Response does not clearly record any additional verification performed during review.
